title: Sample preparation protocol for targeted mass spectrometry analysis of pRabs, total Rabs, LRRK2-pS910, pS935 and total LRRK2
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1che8","text":"Leucine rich repeat kinase2 (LRRK2) is one of the most commonly implicated kinase and mutations that hyperactivate kinase activity is seen in familial Parkinson’s disease. LRRK2 phosphorylates a sub-set of small Rab GTPase proteins within their Switch-II motif. We recently described a multiplexed targeted mass spectrometry assay to identify and quantify both phosphorylated Rab proteins and well known LRRK2 phosphorylation sites such as LRRK2-pS910/pS935(PMID: 33367571). Here we describe a detailed protocol for the sample preparation of cells/tissues that are derived from LRRK2 mutation knock-in models. We outline a simple Triton-X 100 or SDS lysis that are straightforward to capture solubilized proteins amenable for both immunoblotting and mass spectrometry analysis. A stepwise protocol to buffer exchange Triton-X 100/SDS using S-Trap columns and on-column trypsin digestion. We also describe an optimized condition to conjugate required antibodies for the selective enrichment of pRabs and LRRK2 phosphopeptides and total LRRK2 peptide including the protocol for peptide immunoprecipitation and subsequent Stage tip-based desalting of the samples prior to targeted mass spectrometry analysis. We provide a cautionary step’s in each sections of the protocol for a reproducible sample preparation. We believe this protocol is an easy handout to study LRRK2 activity both from cells/tissues including samples from human neutrophils.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Snap freeze mouse tissue immediately after isolation in liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C .

Pulverize frozen tissue in liquid nitrogen to a fine powder and snap freeze immediately in 2ml tubes. This is done using cell crusher kit. Maintain all Pulverizer units in liquid nitrogen including spatula. After use wash the pulveriser with a tap water and clean thoroughly several times with 70 % (v/v), ethanol .

Weigh pulverized tissue powder on a weighing balance. 5-7% of the wet weight of tissue should correspond to a protein amount of ~ 5 mg to 7 mg, protein / 100 mg, wet weight . Keep samples on dry ice during and after weighing. Take a 1.5 ml tube check the weight on weighing balance and subsequently tare the weight. Now take small scoop of sample using pre-chilled fine spatula to weigh the sample
Immediately add 10 X, weight of the tissue of 2 Mass Percent, SDS lysis buffer . For e.g. 100 mg, tissue powder please add 1000 µL, lysis buffer .
Put samples on an orbital shaker in cold room at 1000 rpm, °C, 15 minutes .
Boil samples at 95 °C for 00:10:00 and allow to cool.
Sonicate samples using a Diagenode Bioruptor (at high energy for 10 cycles ( 00:00:30, ON / 00:00:30, OFF )).
Clarify lysate by centrifuging at 20800 rpm, 4 °C, 30 minutes .
Measure protein amount using the Bicinchoninic acid assay (BCA) method in triplicate at 1:10 and 1:20 dilutions-repeat analysis if readings are not close.

Take 300 µg, protein amount for Mass spectrometry analysis. In case of mice brain tissues the minimum recommended lysate amount is 1 mg to identify and quantify endogenous pRab proteins and LRRK2 pS910/pS935 and total LRRK2 peptide.

For cells such as A549 or HEK293 or Mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs): Culture them in 6cm or 10cm dish to a ~70% confluency and complete the required treatments such as MLi2 treatment for about 01:30:00 .
Harvest cells by washing with 1X plain DMEM media without any serum and aspirate the media completely.
Lyse cells using 1% Triton-X 100 lysis buffer. Add 150 µL to a 6cm dish or 350 µL to a 10cm dish.
Scrape cells using cell scraper and collect the lysate using 1 ml non-autoclaved tips into a protein lo binding tubes.
Keep cells On ice for 00:30:00 and centrifuge at 18000 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes .
Estimate protein amounts using BCA assay.
Take 300 µg, protein amount for Mass spectrometry analysis (cell lysate or tissue lysate).
Aliquot 300 µg, protein amount in 1.5 ml Protein lo-binding tubes.
Perform reduction by adding 1:10 dilution of a solution of 0.1 M TCEP dissolved in 300 mM TEABC to bring final concentration to 10 millimolar (mM), TCEP .
Incubate on a Thermomixer with a gentle agitation at 1000 rpm, 60 °C, 30 minutes .

Bring tubes to Room temperature and add a 1:10 dilution of freshly prepared 0.4 Molarity (M), iodoacetamide dissolved in water. It is critical that the samples are at room temperature prior to addition of iodoacetamide.
Incubate in dark on a Thermomixer with a gentle agitation at 1000 rpm, Room temperature, 30 minutes .

Quench alkylation by addition of a 1:10 dilution of a solution of 0.1 M TCEP dissolved in 300 mM TEABC to bring final concentration to 10 millimolar (mM), TCEP . If samples are not quenched after alkylation proceed immediately to the next steps below. At this stage sample can be stored in -80 °C freezer.
Incubate on a Thermomixer with a gentle agitation at 1000 rpm, Room temperature, 20 minutes .

Add SDS to a final concentration of 5 Mass Percent from 20 Mass Percent, SDS stock . The lysate is already in 2% (by mass) SDS, so supplement with a stock of 20% (by mass) SDS in order to bring the final SDS concentration to 5% (by mass). For lysates with 1% Triton-x 100, it is important to add final 5% (by mass) SDS to the samples to ensure complete denaturation and subsequent capturing of fine protein particles by S-Trap columns.
Add a 1:10 dilution of 12 % volume, phosphoric acid into the sample to make a final concentration of ~ 1.2 % volume, phosphoric acid . Note for example, 5 µL to 50 µL .

Dilute the sample 7 times in S-Trap wash buffer ( 90 % volume, methanol in 0.1 Molarity (M), TEAB pH 7.1 v/v ). For example, if sample volume is 50 µL then add 300 µL, S-Trap wash buffer ( 90 % volume, methanol in 0.1 Molarity (M), TEAB pH 7.1 v/v ). If the lysate volume is more than 50 µl, scale S-Trap wash buffer accordingly i.e. 7 times the volume of the lysate
Prepare an S-Trap mini column in a 2 ml tube.
Add the diluted protein mixture from step 27 to the column.

Centrifuge briefly at 1000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute to capture the protein particles. All centrifugation steps should be at room temperature. If you observe any clogging then increase the centrifugation speed up to a maximum of 4,000g or increase the centrifugation time up to 2-4 minutes. . If the total volume of Lysate +S-Trap buffer mixture is >500 µl then add it to the column multiple times until the sample passes through the column and discard flow through

Wash column with 400 µL, S-Trap buffer for a total of 4 times (spin 1000 x g, °C, 1 minute between washes): Note that the protein remains bound on the column and SDS and buffer components that effect trypsin digestion are removed.
Wash column with 400 µL, S-Trap buffer Spin 1000 x g, °C, 1 minute (1/4)
Wash column with 400 µL, S-Trap buffer Spin 1000 x g, °C, 1 minute (2/4)
Wash column with 400 µL, S-Trap buffer Spin 1000 x g, °C, 1 minute (3/4)
Wash column with 400 µL, S-Trap buffer Spin 1000 x g, °C, 1 minute (4/4)
Move the S-Trap column to a clean 2 ml tube for digestion.

Add 160 µL, freshly dissolved trypsin solution containing 15 µg, trypsin dissolved in 100 millimolar (mM), TEAB (1:20) . Prepare and store Trypsin stocks in Milli-Q water at 5 µg/µL of 50µl vials and store in -20 °C freezer.
Centrifuge briefly at 200 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Collect flow through and reapply the trypsin solution back onto the column being careful to avoid air bubbles.
Cap the tubes and incubate at 47 °C without shaking for 01:30:00 . Do not shake as this causes bubbles and damages the column.
Incubate for another 02:00:00 at Room temperature .
Add 80 µL, 50 mM TEAB then spin to elute. Place the eluate in a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube termed “eluate tube”.
Next, add 80 µL, 0.15% (by vol) Formic Acid and spin to elute. Also add this eluate to the “eluate tube”.
Finally, add 80 µL, 50% (by vol) Acetonitrile in 0.15% (by vol) formic acid and spin to elute. Also add this eluate to the “eluate tube”. 3 eluates should have been added to the eluate tube.

Take 1 µL - 2 µL, combined eluate , vacuum dry and inject on MS to verify the digestion efficiency. Analyse data with a 70 min gradient run on QE HF-X or Orbitrap Lumos mass spectrometer in a F1-T-FT-HCD mode. Search data with Proteome Discoverer 2.1 or 2.4 version. Determine the digestion efficiency by plotting number of missed cleavages. Zero missed cleavages should be >75% and single missed cleavages should be between 20-23%
Estimate peptide amounts using CBQCA assay kit and take equal amounts of peptide digest for each sample and Vacuum dry using SpeedVac. Aliquot 5 µg, peptide digest separately for total Rab analysis.

Take required amount of Protein A/G beads and wash with a total of 1 mL, slurry volume + reminder is PBS wash volume (1X PBS buffer) . Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, 3 minutes . (e.g. For 24 samples with 2 rounds of immunoprecipitations 480 µL, bead slurry is required. 10 µL, slurry /sample for one round of immunoprecipitation). Let the beads settle down for 00:02:00 before aspirating the supernatant.
Repeat wash steps with 1 mL, 1X PBS two more times:
Wash beads with 1 mL, 1X PBS . (1/2)
Wash beads with 1 mL, 1X PBS . (2/2)

Incubate an equimolar concentration of Pan-pRab, UDD1, UDD2 and 8G10 antibodies to give a concentration of 50 ng/ul of slurry. (Example, for 24 samples with two round of Immunoprecipitation, you require 24 µg, Pan-pRab + 24 µg, UDD1 + 24 µg, UDD2 + 24 µg, 8G10 in a total of 480 µL, protein A/G slurry which will give 50ng/µl concentration). Due to the high binding capacity of Protein A/G beads all four antibodies can be simultaneously conjugated with 25 µg can be multiplexed to conjugate with 500 µL, Protein A/G beads slurry . For 24 samples 12 µg of each of the antibodies are required i.e. 0.5 µg per sample
Add extra 1X PBS to a final volume of 1 mL .
Incubate at 4 °C for 03:00:00 on an end-to-end rotator.

Post incubation, centrifuge at 2000 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes and collect the supernatant to be used as a flow-through fraction to confirm by SDS-PAGE that the antibodies are bound to the beads completely.
Wash with 1 mL, 1X PBS for a total of 3 times:
Wash with 1 mL, 1X PBS . (1/3)
Wash with 1 mL, 1X PBS . (2/3)
Wash with 1 mL, 1X PBS . (3/3)

Now add 1X PBS to a final volume of 480 µL to achieve an antibody concentration of 50 ng/ul, beads slurry . This mixture is stable at 4 °C for up to two months. For a longer storage add 0.03 % volume, sodium azide .

Heavy/light synthetic peptides generally come in a lyophilized form at a net amount of 1 nanomolar (nM) . Our lab has pRab1, pRab3, pRab8, pRab10, pRab35 and pRab43 and LRRK2 pS910 and pS935 and total LRRK2 heavy synthetic peptides.

Dissolve peptides in 100 µL, reconstitution buffer . Leave tubes On ice for about 00:20:00 and then sonicate using a water bath sonicator for 00:05:00 . Reconstitution buffer: 3 % volume, Acetonitrile in 0.1 % volume, formic acid in HPLC-grade water to achieve 10 pmol/µl.
Vortex and centrifuge tubes using benchtop microfuge for 00:00:30 .

Now prepare dilutions using reconstitution buffer as required (e.g. 10pmol/ µl, 1pmol/µl, 100 fmol/µl and 10fmol/µl.) From these stocks prepare an equimolar concentration of all six pRabs+3 LRRK2 peptides to get 1pmol/µl or 100fmol/µl or 10fmol/µl concentrations to be Spiked into the peptide mixture just before immunoprecipitation. Store these stocks in -80 °C freezer. For example, to achieve 1000 fmol/µl of an equimolar concentration of 200 µl final volume, take 20 µl from each peptide (6 Rabs+3LRRK2 peptides) from a stock of 10 pmol/µl to a total volume of 160 µl and then add 40 µl of reconstitution buffer to achieve a final concentration of 1000fmol/µl in 200 µl volume.

Maintain 10 fmol/µl as working stocks so that 10 µL should give 100 fmol net amount in immunoprecipitation reaction. To avoid pipetting errors it is recommended to maintain the desired amount that will be spiked into the peptide mixture to be at a minimum of 10µl. e.g. For 100 fmol add 10µl of 10 fmol/µl stock. If you intend to Spike 50 fmol amount, then add 10 µl of 5fmol/µl stock

Spin the lyophilized peptides from Step 42, at 17000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes to ensure everything settles down at the bottom of the tube. Add 1 mL, 1X IAP buffer , keep samples on water bath sonication for 00:10:00 . The pH should be around pH 7.2 . Use pH strips that have a fine gradient scale from pH 1.0 to pH 10, to adjust the pH

If the digest is still acidic, adjust the pH by adding 5 µL, 1 M Tris pH 12.0 . Centrifuge at high speed at Room temperature for 00:10:00 to remove any insoluble traces and transfer supernatant to new 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube.

Keep samples On ice . In the meantime, aliquot required amount of antibody slurry from the stock as prepared in Step 49. For each peptide immunoprecipitation containing up to 100 µg - 150 µg, tryptic peptide we use 10 µL, slurry containing 0.5 µg, Pan-pRab + 0.5 µg, UDD1 + 0.5 µg, UDD2 + 0.5 µg, 8G10 antibody. Also, add antibody cocktail using nonautoclaved cut tips. Due to low abundance of pRabs+LRRK2 pS910, pS935 in brain the recommended peptide amount to be used is >600 µg peptide digest derived from 1 to 1.5mg starting lysate.

Add 100 fmol amount of an equimolar heavy stable peptides derived from step 55. We use an equimolar mixture of 10µl of 10fmol/µl stock to achieve 100 fmol amount containing six pRabs and LRRK2 pS910, pS9135 and unmodified peptide
Incubate the mixture at 4 °C for 02:00:00 on an end-to-end rotator.
After the incubation, centrifuge at 1500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes . Collect the flow through and transfer it to new protein lo-binding 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes for second round of immunoprecipitation.
Keep beads on an ice-block On ice until the second round of immunoprecipitation is completed.

To the flowthrough from the 1st round of immunoprecipitation (Step 60), add another 10 µL, antibody slurry containing 0.5 µg, Pan-pRab + 0.5 µg, UDD1 + 0.5 µg, UDD2 + 0.5 µg, 8G10 antibody and continue with the second round of immunoprecipitation for another 02:00:00 at 4 °C on an end-to-end rotator.

Centrifuge at 1500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes . Collect the flow through and transfer it to new 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes. This flow through can also be used for subsequent enrichment of a different analyte or second round of IP or phosphopeptides using TiO2.
Add 400 µL, 1X IAP buffer to the beads and carefully transfer it to the beads that are kept On ice from 1st round of immunoprecipitation in step 61.

Add another 600 µL, 1X IAP buffer to the beads and gently wash tubes by inverting up-and-down for about 20 times. This can be easily done by keeping samples on an ice-block and cover it with silver foil. Now complete wash by inverting up-and-down steps for all tubes at once. Use this technique for all subsequent wash steps.
Centrifuge tubes at 1500 x g, 4 °C, 3 minutes . Place tubes on ice block and allow the beads to settle for at least 00:02:00 before aspirating the supernatant.

Repeat washing by adding 1 mL, ice cold 1X IAP buffer and centrifuge tubes at 1500 x g, 4 °C, 3 minutes . Place tubes on ice block and allow the beads to settle for at least 00:02:00 before aspirating the supernatant.

Wash the beads with 1 mL, ice-cold 1X PBS , Centrifuge tubes at 1500 x g, 4 °C, 3 minutes . Place tubes on ice block and allow the beads to settle for at least 00:02:00 before aspirating the supernatant. (1/2)

Wash the beads with 1 mL, ice-cold 1X PBS , Centrifuge tubes at 1500 x g, 4 °C, 3 minutes . Place tubes on ice block and allow the beads to settle for at least 00:02:00 before aspirating the supernatant. (2/2)

Wash the beads with 1 mL, ice-cold Milli-Q water , Centrifuge tubes at 1500 x g, 4 °C, 3 minutes . Place tubes on ice block and allow the beads to settle for at least 00:02:00 before aspirating the supernatant.

After the final wash, centrifuge and aspirate all of the Milli-Q water and add 50 µL, 0.2% (by vol) TFA to the beads. Gently tap for proper mixing and incubate on a Thermo mixer at 1250 rpm, Room temperature, 10 minutes .
Centrifuge at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes and collect the eluate into a new collection tube with a gel loader tip.

Repeat the elution step by adding another 50 µL, 0.2% (by vol) TFA followed by incubation at Room temperature for 00:10:00 and centrifuge and collect the supernatant into the previous collection tube and proceed with C18 stage-tip clean up.

Prepare single layer of C18 stage-tip using 16-gauge syringe needle as shown in figure 2. Fig. 2) Homemade C18 stage-tip assembly: A) Punch a C18 layer using 16 gauge needle B) Push the C18 layer into a 200 µl pipette tip using a spray duster C) Stage the tip using PTFE-O ring into a 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube for desalting.
Add 80 µL, 100% (by vol) Acetonitrile to the C18 stage-tip. Note this is required to activate the C18 resin.
Centrifuge at 2000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes . Discard flow through.
Add 80 µL, Solvent-A1 0.1% (by vol) TFA (by vol) . Note this is required to equilibrate the C18 resin.
Centrifuge at 2000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes . Discard flow through. (1/2)
Centrifuge at 2000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes . Discard flow through. (2/2)
Load the acidified peptide digest from step 72 to Stage-tip.
Centrifuge at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes .
Reapply the flow through to the C18 stage-tip column and repeat this step. (1/2)
Reapply the flow through to the C18 stage-tip column. (2/2)
Add 80 µL, solvent-A1 (0.1% (by vol) TFA v/v) , and centrifuge at 2000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes . Discard flow through. Repeat again. (1/2)
Add 80 µL, solvent-A1 (0.1% (by vol) TFA v/v) , and centrifuge at 2000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes . Discard flow through. (2/2)
Elute peptides absorbed to C18 column by placing the stage-tips into new 1.5 ml low binding tubes.
Add 30 µL, Elution buffer (Solvent B1- 50% (by vol) acetonitrile in 0.1% (by vol) TFA) .
Centrifuge at 1500 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Add another 30 µL, Elution buffer (Solvent B1- 50% (by vol) acetonitrile in 0.1% (by vol) TFA) and repeat the centrifugation at 1500 x g, °C, 2 minutes .
Immediately snap freeze on dry ice the eluates and vacuum dry the samples using a Speed-Vac.
Store samples in -80 °C freezer until the LC-MS/MS analysis.
